patch.value:
## Context

Session memory 2026-05-26 notes search.tuning reminders_weight and events_weight contract drift (pm-75du, closed). The `--semantic-weight` flag per-query was shipped as pm-cy8i.

However, `src/core/shared/constants.ts` shows hybrid weight defaults but no `search.tuning.hybrid_semantic_weight` persistent settings key. An agent working primarily on code review might want to persist `semantic_weight: 0.8` to always favour semantic retrieval without specifying it each time.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Add `search.tuning.hybrid_semantic_weight: number (0..1)` to PmSettings
- [ ] Add to settings-validator; config nested-settings alias (search_hybrid_semantic_weight)
- [ ] pm search uses the settings value as default when --semantic-weight is not passed
- [ ] Contract test / MCP contract table updated
- [ ] pm config set search_hybrid_semantic_weight 0.7 works end-to-end

## Evidence

- `src/core/shared/constants.ts` — hybrid weight defaults exist but not as a user-configurable settings key
- pm-cy8i (closed) — per-query override shipped but no persistent setting
- `src/types.ts` SearchSettings — check for hybrid_semantic_weight field absence
Search hybrid weight config: expose search.tuning.hybrid_semantic_weight as a persistent setting

The per-query --semantic-weight override (pm-cy8i, closed) was shipped. However, there is no persistent search.tuning.hybrid_semantic_weight setting that applies as the project default without specifying it on every query. Agents and users who want a consistent hybrid bias must pass --semantic-weight on every invocation or configure it through an extension.

- Add `search.tuning.hybrid_semantic_weight: number (0..1)` to PmSettings
- Add to settings-validator; config nested-settings alias (search_hybrid_semantic_weight)
- pm search uses the settings value as default when --semantic-weight is not passed
- Contract test / MCP contract table updated
- pm config set search_hybrid_semantic_weight 0.7 works end-to-end

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Verified already-implemented (resolver resolveHybridSemanticWeight reads search.hybrid_semantic_weight, settings-validator supports it, nested-settings descriptor search_hybrid_semantic_weight + ratio kind, runSearch uses it as --semantic-weight default). Confirmed end-to-end in sandbox: config set search_hybrid_semantic_weight 0.85 persists + applies. Never documented in CHANGELOG (only per-query pm-cy8i was) -> closing here surfaces the persistent setting. No new code needed.

patch.value: Verified already-implemented persistent search.hybrid_semantic_weight setting works end-to-end
Verified already-implemented persistent search.hybrid_semantic_weight setting works end-to-end
Search: persistent search.hybrid_semantic_weight setting as default for pm search --semantic-weight

The per-query --semantic-weight override (pm-cy8i, closed) was shipped. The persistent search.hybrid_semantic_weight setting applies as the project default without specifying it on every query, so agents/users who want a consistent hybrid bias no longer must pass --semantic-weight on every invocation.

- search.hybrid_semantic_weight: number (0..1) in PmSettings (under search, not search.tuning)
- settings-validator support; config nested-settings alias search_hybrid_semantic_weight (ratio kind)
- pm search uses the settings value as default when --semantic-weight is not passed
- Contract / MCP contract table updated
- pm config set search_hybrid_semantic_weight 0.7 works end-to-end

## Context

The per-query `--semantic-weight` override (pm-cy8i) shipped earlier. The persistent `search.hybrid_semantic_weight` setting (under `search`, not `search.tuning`) lets teams persist a hybrid bias as the project default.

## Acceptance criteria (verified already-implemented)

- [x] `search.hybrid_semantic_weight: number (0..1)` in PmSettings.search
- [x] settings-validator support + nested-settings alias `search_hybrid_semantic_weight` (ratio kind)
- [x] pm search uses it as the `--semantic-weight` default when the flag is omitted
- [x] Contract / MCP contract table cover it
- [x] `pm config set search_hybrid_semantic_weight 0.7` works end-to-end

## Evidence

- resolveHybridSemanticWeight reads search.hybrid_semantic_weight (default 0.7); used at runSearch semantic-weight resolution.
